message:
fix(net): handle NotConnected errors with fallback poll and add vectored write for unix stream split

All socket types (TCP listener, UDP, Unix datagram, Unix listener, Unix
stream) now treat io::ErrorKind::NotConnected the same as Unsupported
during interest registration — falling back to poll-based wakeups
instead of propagating the error. This prevents spurious failures on
platforms where a socket's connection state can change between the
readiness check and the registration call.

Unix stream split halves (both borrowed WriteHalf and OwnedWriteHalf)
now implement poll_write_vectored and report is_write_vectored() = true,
enabling scatter-gather I/O through the async write interface. This
avoids unnecessary buffer copies when callers already have data in
multiple IoSlice buffers.

Minor: renamed shadowed variable from `e` to `err` in OwnedWriteHalf
for consistency with the rest of the net module.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
